headline: Software Stocks Are Souring Again. Why ‘HALO’ Companies Might Not Be a Better Bet.
summary: Bloom Energy  and  Vertiv  stocks, by contrast, are up roughly 67%.  Data center infrastructure will definitely become more capital intensive as AI expands, and companies that can deliver power and engineering firms that can execute stand to benefit.  Not every power producer will become a growth stock.

headline: Why AI Won’t Kill the Entry-Level Job
summary: ServiceNow CFO Gina Mastantuono pushes back against predictions of entry-level job losses due to AI. She explains why recent college grads are uniquely positioned to thrive, using their AI fluency to build custom digital agents and solve complex bottlenecks.

headline: Software is sick again today... and it’s not just because of Claude
summary: Investing.com -- Software stocks are notably weaker again on Tuesday, and all signs point to Claude's latest release, which allows users' computers to run tasks in their browsers, as the culprit. However, according to one market watcher, six other factors could be weighing on the sector today as well.

headline: Getting Value from AI Today
summary: For many CFOs, value from generative AI has been elusive. Gina Mastantuono, ServiceNow President and CFO, and Harmit Singh, Levi Strauss & Co. Chief Financial and Growth Officer, discuss their vision of successful AI integration and how it could transform a workforce.

headline: ServiceNow and Salesforce Fall 5%: Is the Market Mispricing Both NOW and CRM?
summary: ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) stock and Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) stock are both sliding roughly 5% in Tuesday’s session, a simultaneous drop that puts two of enterprise software’s most prominent names in the same uncomfortable spotlight. headline: Should You Invest in ServiceNow (NOW) Based on Bullish Wall Street Views?
